metadata.title:
KEEN COMPETITION BETWEEN


RIVAL LAMP POST DESIGNERS

Architects K. K Cutter and J. M Goodwin Both Have
Electrolier and There May Be Trouble Before Spo-


kane Decides Definitely on One the Other.

pages.raw_text:
Trouble is promised before Spo-
kane finally settles the street light
ing question and gets all the prop
erty holders lined up for the same
kind of electrolier. While the COM
mittee of the chamber of commerce
supposed to have the power to Sc
lect the kind of lamp post to be
used exclusively has decided on
what is known as the Cutter design,
the hackers of the Goodwin designs
have not laid down, and as the
Goodwin people have about so per
cent of the merchants and property
holders along Riverside avenue al
ready signed up on contracts for
that design, it looks as though there
is going to be two kinds of lamp
posts put up.


Keen rivalry between the design
ers of the two kinds of posts has
marked the street lighting move
ment from the first The Cutter
post, recommended by Architect X.
K. Cutter, is declared by j. M. Good
win, the designer of the other post,
to be copied from the catalog of the
j. l.. Mott Iron Works of New York
He says it is post No. 2005J in the
1909 catalog of that company, and
charges Cutter with filching the
idea bodily. On the other hand he
claims his own design is entirely
original and would make a dis
tinctively Spokane electrolier.


Another claim made by Goodwin
for his post is that it will be made
in Spokane, while the Cutter de-
sign will be made in Minneapolis
Goodwin says the throwing of the
plum by the chamber committee to
Cutter and the Minneapolis firm is
a frameup. The Cutter post will
cost $115 set up ready to light on
the sidewalk, according to the Min
neapolis firms bid, but Goodwin
took the wind out of the committee
the other day by offering to con-
struct the same post in Spokane
and set it up on the sidewalk for
STA.


The Goodwin bid was 1925 per
electrolier set up on the sidewalk,
but this bid included the obtaining
of the contracts from all the prop
erty holders, a considerable work
which must be paid for by some
one. And for that money Goodwin
says his post will be better, be-
cause he says he does not have to
figure in freight from Minneapolis.


Some of the rival posts can be
h seen already set up. The Goodwin
! post can be seen on the south side
Of Riverside avenue between Stew
ems and Howard, and the Cutter de.
sign in front of Davenport's cafe.


The chamber as body has not
acted on the last report of the light
! ing committee, which recommends
/ that action be urged on the city
! council at once to prevent the Good
win design from being put in. The
committee wants the council to de
! cree that the Cutter design shall
be the design Of the city. 1t has
| been ascertained that the council
has no power to create lighting
district and assess property for the
mprovement-the work must be
paid for by the individual property


holders, and the chamber can mere


ly recommend the kind of a lamp t.
! be put in.


1t is expected that the Goodwin
